touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn message sent to Ezra Watson introducing AskElephant's forecasting capabilities. No response received.
Follow-up LinkedIn message highlighting ROI case study from similar-sized SaaS company. No response received.
Brief qualification call with Ezra. He expressed frustration with current forecast accuracy issues impacting board reporting. Confirmed budget authority and timeline interest for Q2 implementation.

questions_asked.question: What's driving your interest in improving forecast accuracy right now?
questions_asked.answer: Our board keeps asking why our numbers shift 20-30% week to week. It's becoming a credibility issue.

questions_asked.question: Who else would be involved in evaluating a solution like this?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, it'd probably just be me and maybe our VP of Ops. I own the sales tools budget.

next_steps: Schedule discovery call for next week
Ezra to review current forecasting process metrics

touches.ai_summary: Deep dive discovery call. Ezra walked through their current spreadsheet-based forecasting process and pain points with data quality. Discussed team size (8 AEs) and need for both automation and some initial setup help. He seemed engaged but mentioned budget constraints.

questions_asked.question: Does your platform integrate with our CRM? We're on HubSpot.
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have a native HubSpot integration that syncs automatically.

questions_asked.question: What's your current process for generating weekly forecasts?
questions_asked.answer: Each AE updates a shared spreadsheet. I manually compile it and try to apply gut-feel adjustments. Takes about 4 hours every Friday.

questions_asked.question: How long does typical implementation take?
questions_asked.answer: With the consulting package, usually 2-3 weeks to full deployment.

objections: We're a small team, so budget is tight. Need to see clear ROI.
next_steps: Schedule demo focused on forecasting dashboards
Send ROI calculator and case study
Ezra to compile current accuracy metrics for comparison

touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent with ROI calculator and link to SaaS forecasting case study. Ezra responded positively, confirmed demo time.

questions_asked.question: Can you include the consulting hours breakdown in the demo?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, I'll walk through the implementation plan and included consulting scope.

touches.ai_summary: Product demo focused on forecasting automation and reporting features. Ezra was impressed with the AI-driven accuracy improvements and dashboard visualizations. Asked detailed questions about data requirements and consulting support. Expressed concern about getting team adoption.

questions_asked.question: What data do you need from our CRM to get started?
questions_asked.answer: Mainly deal stage history, close dates, and any custom fields you use for pipeline categorization.

questions_asked.question: How much training does the team need?
questions_asked.answer: Most AEs are up and running in one session. The consulting package includes team onboarding.

questions_asked.question: What would success look like for you in the first quarter?
questions_asked.answer: If we could get forecast variance under 10% and save me those Friday afternoons, that's a win.

objections: I'm worried my AEs will resist another tool. They're already complaining about HubSpot.
next_steps: Schedule technical eval to review HubSpot integration
Send proposal with automations + consulting package
Ezra to discuss internally with VP of Ops

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation session (rescheduled from previous week). Walked through HubSpot integration setup, data mapping, and security protocols. Ezra's IT contact joined briefly to confirm SSO requirements. Discussion revealed limited executive visibility into the purchase - Ezra mentioned he'd 'loop in leadership after we nail down pricing.'

questions_asked.question: Do you support SSO with Okta?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, SSO via Okta is included in all plans.

questions_asked.question: Has your VP of Ops had a chance to review the proposal?
questions_asked.answer: Not yet - I wanted to make sure the technical fit was solid first. I'll bring her in once we're aligned on pricing.

questions_asked.question: Can we do a phased rollout, maybe start with 4 seats?
questions_asked.answer: We recommend starting with the full team for accurate forecasting, but we can discuss options.

next_steps: Ezra to review proposal and prepare budget justification
Schedule exec meeting to present value proposition
Send security documentation for IT review

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting included Ezra but no other executives attended. Ezra explained the VP of Ops was traveling and CEO delegates all sales tools decisions to him. He presented the business case but seemed uncertain about budget approval. Conversation shifted quickly to pricing flexibility.

questions_asked.question: Would it be helpful to reschedule with your VP of Ops present?
questions_asked.answer: No need - I have the authority to move forward. But I do need to make sure the numbers work for our budget cycle.

questions_asked.question: What's your flexibility on pricing? The $9,200 list price is above what I budgeted.
questions_asked.answer: Let me discuss with my team and see what we can do. What budget range were you targeting?

objections: The price is higher than I expected, especially for a team our size.
next_steps: Rep to explore discount options and get approval
Schedule pricing call for end of week
Ezra to confirm final seat count and implementation timeline

touches.ai_summary: Pricing negotiation call. Ezra pushed for a discount citing budget constraints and competitive evaluation (though no competitor was mentioned previously). Rep offered 31.6% discount to close at $6,300 for 8 seats. Ezra accepted pending legal review of contract terms. He seemed relieved but emphasized need to close quickly to hit Q2 start date.

questions_asked.question: Can we lock in this price for a multi-year deal?
questions_asked.answer: This pricing is for an annual contract. We can discuss multi-year renewal terms after you see the value in year one.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for legal review?
questions_asked.answer: Should be quick - we use standard SaaS terms. I'll push to have it back in 3-5 days.

next_steps: Ezra to route to legal for review
Plan implementation kickoff for first week of Q2

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with executed contract and implementation timeline. Ezra responded confirming legal is reviewing and he expects signature by end of week. Expressed enthusiasm about getting started and improving forecast accuracy.
